rulings.Case Title: JOHNA PECOT et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Continued Special Mtn To Strike Slapp Application For Award Of Attorney Fees Of Mulitiplier Of Two Against Johna Pecot, Thomas Arata, Tich Owyang, Stephen Tilton, Joseph Leake And Oscar Taylor
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 10, 2012, Line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID WONG, MICHAEL ZEHNER, BRIAN SAVAGE, SHEDRICK MCDANIELS Motion For Continued Special Motion To Strike Slapp Application For Award Of Attorney Fees Of Mulitiplier Of Two Against Johna Pecot, Thomas Arata, Tich Owyang, Stephen Tilton, Joseph Leake And Oscar Taylor. Granted in part. Attorney fees of $20,000 are awarded in favor of defendants David Wong, Michael Zehner Brian Savage, Shedrick McDaniels and the San Francisco Deputy Sheriff's Foundation and against the plaintiffs to be paid by March 30, 2012. As the prevailing party in the anti-SLAPP motion, the defendants are entitled to recover their attorney fees and costs in bringing the motion. However, the amount of the fees sought far exceeds what was reasonable to pursue the motion. Based on the court's own review of the motion, an award of $20,000 is reasonable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PANDA RESTAURANT GROUP, INC. et al VS. PILLSBURY WINTHROP SHAW PITTMAN, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, Llp, Paula Weber'S Motion To Re-Open Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 10, 2012, Line 6. Defendant Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman, Llp, Paula Weber'S Motion To Re-Open Depositions. The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles as follows: The Motion to Re-Open Depositions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Plaintiff placed the communications of Mr. Wilkinson related to the guaranteed compensation program at issue and therefore impliedly waived the attorney-client privilege with regard to those communications. The depositions of the following witnesses are re-opened to permit questioning regarding these communications: Wilkinson, Liu, Cherng, Davin, Brant and Chung. No waiver under Evidence Code §958. The discovery cut-off date is now March 23, 2012 solely for the purpose of permitting these depositions to occur and not for any other discovery purpose. The Court invites the Plaintiff to move for reconsideration of the prior ruling of Judge Giorgi with regard to documents related to these same communications. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LATOYA ZENO VS. BLOOD CENTERS OF THE PACIFIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Relief From Order And Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 10, 2012, Line 7. PLAINTIFF LATOYA ZENO Motion For Relief From Order And Judgment. The court treats the motion as seeking relief from the prior order and, if such relief is granted, reconsideration of the prior order based on Ms. Zeno's declaration. So treated, the motion for relief is granted on the condition that Ms. Zeno pay the reasonable attorney's fees incurred by defendant in opposing this motion and the motion for new trial, neither of which would have been necessary but for the late submission of the declaration. The parties are directed to meet and confer in an effort to reach an agreement as to the amount of those fees. Ms. Zeno's failure to submit her declaration on a timely basis was a mistake and/or excusable neglect entitling her to relief. Upon reconsideration of the prior order based on Ms. Zeno's declaration, that declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant had notice of Ms. Zeno's claimed disability and thus the court vacates that prior order and substitutes a new order denying defendant's motion for summary judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN J. DAVIS JR. VS. SCHINDLER ELEVATOR CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 10, 2012, Line 9. DEFENDANT OTIS ELEVATOR COMPANY AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. The Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the first and second causes of action and denied as to the third cause of action. Plaintiff concedes the lack of merit of his first two claims. As to the third claim, Defendant owed a duty to maintain the elevator in a non-negligent manner. If Defendant maintained the elevator during the contract of maintenance in a negligent manner such that the elevator posed an unreasonable risk of harm, Defendant could still be liable for an injury resulting from that unreasonable risk even if the injury did not occur until after the contract for maintenance was terminated. Moreover, Plaintiff raised a triable issue whether Defendant had the ability to make repairs and therefore exercised control over the maintenance of the elevator even after the contract was terminated by virtue of the warranty. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WESLEY STERMAN VS. NICHOLAS COLYVAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Supplemental Production To Pltf'S Requests For Production Of Documents;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 10, 2012, Line 20. PLAINTIFF WESLEY STERMAN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Supplemental Production To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents. The court adopts the recommendation and reasoning of Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, which is to deny the motion and requests for sanctions as follows: The simple denial of a contention in litigation is not a waiver of a privilege. Production of the 2005 - 2007 tax returns does not establish a waiver of the privilege for the 2008-2010 tax records.
